([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): [อำเภอ](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%B3%E0%B9%80%E0%B8%A0%E0%B8%AD "wikt:อำเภอ"), [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Thai "Romanization of Thai"): *amphơ̄*, IPA: [[ʔāmpʰɤ̄ː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai"); 'district'), also anglicised as **amphur** ([/ˈæmfɜːr/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") or [/ˈæmpɜːr/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")), is the second level administrative subdivision of [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"). Groups of *amphoe* or districts make up the [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand"), and are analogous to [counties](/wiki/County "County"). The chief district officer is *Nai Amphoe* ([นายอำเภอ](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%B3%E0%B9%80%E0%B8%A0%E0%B8%AD "wikt:นายอำเภอ")). *Amphoe* are divided into *[tambons](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon")* ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): [ตำบล](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%95%E0%B8%B3%E0%B8%9A%E0%B8%A5 "wikt:ตำบล"); 'subdistricts').

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Amphoe](/w/index.php?title=Amphoe&redirect=no "Amphoe"))

Second-level administrative subdivision of Thailand

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2f/District_office%2C_Mae_Sai_-_2017-07-03_%28001%29.jpg/250px-District_office%2C_Mae_Sai_-_2017-07-03_%28001%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:District_office,_Mae_Sai_-_2017-07-03_(001).jpg)

Office of amphoe [Mae Sai](/wiki/Mae_Sai_district "Mae Sai district") in [Chiang Rai province](/wiki/Chiang_Rai_province "Chiang Rai province")

An **amphoe** ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): [อำเภอ](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%B3%E0%B9%80%E0%B8%A0%E0%B8%AD "wikt:อำเภอ"), [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Thai "Romanization of Thai"): *amphơ̄*, IPA: [[ʔāmpʰɤ̄ː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Thai "Help:IPA/Thai"); 'district'), also anglicised as **amphur** ([/ˈæmfɜːr/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") or [/ˈæmpɜːr/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")), is the second level administrative subdivision of [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand"). Groups of *amphoe* or districts make up the [provinces](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand"), and are analogous to [counties](/wiki/County "County"). The chief district officer is *Nai Amphoe* ([นายอำเภอ](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%99%E0%B8%B2%E0%B8%A2%E0%B8%AD%E0%B8%B3%E0%B9%80%E0%B8%A0%E0%B8%AD "wikt:นายอำเภอ")). *Amphoe* are divided into *[tambons](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon")* ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): [ตำบล](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E0%B8%95%E0%B8%B3%E0%B8%9A%E0%B8%A5 "wikt:ตำบล"); 'subdistricts').

Altogether Thailand has 928 districts,[[1]](#cite_note-1) including the 50 [districts of Bangkok](/wiki/Districts_of_Bangkok "Districts of Bangkok"), which are called *[khet](/wiki/Khet_(country_subdivision) "Khet (country subdivision)")* (เขต) since the Bangkok administrative reform of 1972. The number of districts in provinces varies, from only three in the smallest provinces, up to the 50 urban districts of [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok"). Also the sizes and population of districts differ greatly. The smallest population is in [Ko Kut](/wiki/Ko_Kut_district "Ko Kut district") ([Trat province](/wiki/Trat_province "Trat province")) with just 2,042 citizens, while [Mueang Samut Prakan](/wiki/Mueang_Samut_Prakan_district "Mueang Samut Prakan district") ([Samut Prakan province](/wiki/Samut_Prakan_province "Samut Prakan province")) has 509,262 citizens. The *khet* of Bangkok have the smallest areas—Khet [Samphanthawong](/wiki/Samphanthawong_district "Samphanthawong district") is the smallest, with only 1.4 km2—while the *amphoe* of the sparsely populated mountain regions are bigger than some provinces. [Umphang](/wiki/Umphang_district "Umphang district") ([Tak province](/wiki/Tak_province "Tak province")) at 4,325.4 km2 is the largest and also has the lowest population density. The average area of a district in Thailand is about 552.93 km2 (213.49 sq mi), while its average population of a district in Thailand is about 75,345 people.

The names of *amphoe* are usually unique, but in a few cases different Thai names have the same form in English due to the flaws of the [romanization system](/wiki/Royal_Thai_General_System_of_Transcription "Royal Thai General System of Transcription"). The notable exception, however, is the name [Amphoe Chaloem Phra Kiat](/wiki/Chaloem_Phra_Kiat_district_(disambiguation) "Chaloem Phra Kiat district (disambiguation)"), which was given to five districts created in 1996 in celebration of the 50th anniversary of King [Bhumibol Adulyadej](/wiki/Bhumibol_Adulyadej "Bhumibol Adulyadej")'s accession to the throne. *Chaloem Phra Kiat* (เฉลิมพระเกียรติ) means 'in commemoration of' or 'in honour of' a royal family member.

| [Administrative divisions of Thailand](/wiki/Organization_of_the_government_of_Thailand "Organization of the government of Thailand") |
| --- |
| Provincial divisions |
| * [Province](/wiki/Provinces_of_Thailand "Provinces of Thailand") (*Changwat*) * District (*Amphoe*) * [Subdistrict](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon") (*Tambon*) * [Village](/wiki/Muban "Muban") (*Muban*) |
| Local divisions |
| | First-level | | --- | | * [Provincial administrative organisation](/wiki/Provincial_administrative_organization "Provincial administrative organization")  (*Ongkan borihan suan changwat*)  ---  * [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok")†  (*Krung Thep Maha Nakhon*) | | Second-level | | * [City municipality](/wiki/Thesaban#City_municipality "Thesaban")  (*Thesaban nakhon*)  ---  * [Town municipality](/wiki/Thesaban#Town_municipality "Thesaban")  (*Thesaban mueang*)  ---  * [Subdistrict municipality](/wiki/Thesaban#Subdistrict_municipality "Thesaban")  (*Thesaban tambon*)  ---  * Subdistrict administrative organisation  (*Ongkan borihan suan tambon*)  ---  * [Pattaya City](/wiki/Pattaya_City "Pattaya City")†  (*Mueang Pattaya*) | | Subdivisions of Bangkok | | * [District](/wiki/List_of_districts_of_Bangkok "List of districts of Bangkok") (*Khet*) * [Subdistrict](/wiki/Khwaeng "Khwaeng") (*Khwaeng*) | | Subdivisions of City municipality | | * *[Khwaeng](/wiki/Khwaeng "Khwaeng")* | |
| Note: † Special administrative area |
| * [v](/wiki/Template:Administrative_divisions_of_Thailand "Template:Administrative divisions of Thailand") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Administrative_divisions_of_Thailand "Template talk:Administrative divisions of Thailand") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Administrative_divisions_of_Thailand "Special:EditPage/Template:Administrative divisions of Thailand") |

## Local administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Thailand&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Local administration")]

Each district is led by a district chief officer (*nai amphoe*, นายอำเภอ), who is appointed by the [Ministry of Interior](/wiki/Ministry_of_Interior_(Thailand) "Ministry of Interior (Thailand)"). The officer is a subordinate of the provincial governor.

## Amphoe mueang

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Thailand&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Amphoe mueang")]

Further information: [Mueang](/wiki/Mueang "Mueang"), [Muban](/wiki/Muban "Muban"), [Sukhaphiban](/wiki/Sukhaphiban "Sukhaphiban"), [Tambon](/wiki/Tambon "Tambon"), and [Thesaban](/wiki/Thesaban "Thesaban")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Tour_horloge_nuit_violet.jpg/250px-Tour_horloge_nuit_violet.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tour_horloge_nuit_violet.jpg)

The Mueang [Chiang Rai](/wiki/Chiang_Rai "Chiang Rai") city clock tower in the centre of amphoe meaung Chiang Rai

The district which contains the administrative office of the province is the *amphoe mueang* (lit. *town district*). The district is not to be confused with the capital town itself, which is a different administrative entity usually much smaller than the district. Until the 1930s, most of the capital districts had names just like other districts, whereas districts dating back to old provinces had the word *mueang* in their name. In 1938 all the capital districts were renamed *amphoe mueang*, whereas in all non-capital districts *mueang* was removed from the name.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The notable exception to this rule is [Ayutthaya](/wiki/Phra_Nakhon_Si_Ayutthaya_province "Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya province"), where the capital district is named [Amphoe Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya](/wiki/Phra_Nakhon_Si_Ayutthaya_district "Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya district") (instead of *Amphoe Mueang Ayutthaya*), the same as the province, which is fully named *Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya*. Also the capital districts of Thonburi and Phra Nakhon Provinces had the same name as the province, which they kept when the two provinces were merged to form Bangkok metropolis.

In most cases the capital district is also the most populous district of the province, as the provincial administration is usually in the largest town of the province. [Songkhla province](/wiki/Songkhla_province "Songkhla province") is the most striking exception, as the town (and thus also the district) [Hat Yai](/wiki/Hat_Yai "Hat Yai") grew much faster than the capital [Mueang Songkhla](/wiki/Mueang_Songkhla_district "Mueang Songkhla district") due to its better transport connections.

There are four districts in Thailand ([Chan](/wiki/Mueang_Chan_district "Mueang Chan district"), [Pan](/wiki/Mueang_Pan_district "Mueang Pan district"), [Suang](/wiki/Mueang_Suang_district "Mueang Suang district"), and [Yang](/wiki/Mueang_Yang_district "Mueang Yang district")) which contain the term *mueang* in their name as well, even though they are not capital districts. All of these were created relatively recently, between 1973 and 1995.

## King amphoe

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Thailand&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: King amphoe")]

Minor districts (*king amphoe*, กิ่งอำเภอ — กิ่ง literally 'branch') are set up when the administration of areas remote from the district center is inconvenient for citizens. Most of the tasks of the *amphoe* are transferred to the *king amphoe*, but it is still partially a subordinate of the *amphoe* it was created from. When the *king amphoe* meets the necessary qualifications to become an *amphoe*, it is usually promoted. However, not every newly created *amphoe* begins as a *king amphoe*: if the qualifications are met directly, this phase is skipped. While usually a minor district is upgraded after a few years, in some cases it remains a minor district for decades. For example, [Ko Yao](/wiki/Amphoe_Ko_Yao "Amphoe Ko Yao") was a minor district for 85 years until it was upgraded in 1988. Sometimes a district is downgraded to a minor district. [Thung Wa](/wiki/Amphoe_Thung_Wa "Amphoe Thung Wa") lost a lot of its population to neighboring [La-ngu minor district](/wiki/Amphoe_La-ngu "Amphoe La-ngu"), so finally La-ngu was upgraded and Thung Wa downgraded. Another example is [Chumphon Buri](/wiki/Amphoe_Chumphon_Buri "Amphoe Chumphon Buri"), which was reduced after the more developed part was split off to form a new district and the remaining district was downgraded.

The criteria required for an *amphoe* are a population of at least 30,000 people and at least five *tambon*, or, if the area is more than 25 km (16 mi) from the district office, a population of at least 15,000 and four *tambon*.

A minor district is led by a chief officer (*Hua Na King Amphoe*, หัวหน้ากิ่งอำเภอ).

The Thai word *king* (กิ่ง) means 'branch' and should not be confused with the English word "king". The officially recommended translation is "minor district" —however they are also quite commonly translated as sub-district, which is the recommended translation for *tambon*, and also wrongly suggests that they are at a lower administrative level than the *amphoe*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The Thai government upgraded all remaining 81 minor districts to full districts on 15 May 2007 in order to streamline administration.[[4]](#cite_note-4) With publication in the *Royal Gazette* on 24 August the order became official.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## District office

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Districts_of_Thailand&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: District office")]

The administration of the district is housed in an office building called *thi wa kan amphoe* (ที่ว่าการอำเภอ), which also marks the center of each district. Distances on road signs are always calculated to this office building. The office is usually in the largest settlement of the district, to make it easily accessible to the majority of the population —one of the tasks of the amphoe is the [civil registry](/wiki/Civil_registry "Civil registry"), which makes the district the most important of the administrative levels for the Thai public.
